task_completion.description: The user has opened the Segment Editor module in 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the 'Segment Editor' module selected in the module dropdown or the Segment Editor panel is visible on the left side.

task_completion.description: The segment list contains the original Tumor plus three new segments with appropriate names.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Segment Editor table lists 'Tumor' and three other segments named similar to 'Zone1', 'Zone2', 'Zone3' (or containing 5mm, 10mm, 15mm).

task_completion.description: The user utilizes the Margin effect or Logical Operators to modify segment shapes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the 'Margin' effect or 'Logical operators' effect active in the Segment Editor, or the user setting margin sizes (mm).

task_completion.description: Zone 1 is colored red and appears as a ring immediately surrounding the tumor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D slice views or 3D view, a RED band/ring is visible immediately adjacent to the tumor core. It should not cover the tumor core (should be a ring).

task_completion.description: Zone 2 is colored yellow and appears as a ring surrounding Zone 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D slice views or 3D view, a YELLOW band/ring is visible surrounding the red zone. It should be distinct and not overlap the red zone significantly.

task_completion.description: Zone 3 is colored green and appears as a ring surrounding Zone 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D slice views or 3D view, a GREEN band/ring is visible surrounding the yellow zone. It should be the outermost layer.

task_completion.description: The 3D view displays all three concentric zones simultaneously in the correct order (Red -> Yellow -> Green).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D visualization window shows a nested structure: Tumor (center) -> Red -> Yellow -> Green. The segments appear as shells or rings, not just a single large solid blob.

task_completion.description: The segmentation is saved to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the 'Save' dialog or 'Export' settings with the filename 'dose_zones.seg.nrrd' and destination '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/' (or similar valid path).

integrity.description: The user interacts with the 3D Slicer GUI (Segment Editor) rather than scripting the entire solution in the Python console.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with buttons, sliders, and menus in the Segment Editor panel.

integrity.description: The screenshots depict the actual 3D Slicer application interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface includes standard Slicer elements like the module selector, slice views (Red/Yellow/Green windows), and 3D view.

integrity.description: The segmentation is performed on the loaded brain MRI data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The background of the 2D slice views shows the grayscale brain MRI (FLAIR) anatomy, not a blank or generic background.
